dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, {target_npc} refused to see me. Slammed the door and told me to leave before they did something they'd regret.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I expected anger, but the depth of it shook me. {target_npc} said {quest_focus} destroyed more than just their standing: it cost them friends, livelihood, years they'll never get back. They threw the {quest_item} I brought into the dirt and said no gift erases what happened. The {enemy_type} have moved closer to where {target_npc} is sheltering {location_hint}, and if they stay out of stubbornness, they'll be in real danger. I need to find something more meaningful than objects: proof that {quest_stakes} still has value.
dialogueChunks.outro: Words failed. Gifts failed. The only thing left is action that speaks louder than either.

playerResponses.text: Actions over apologies. Tell me what would actually prove you've changed.
Rejected twice. You're persistent. I respect that, even if it's painful.
If they don't want your apology, forcing it makes things worse.

dialogueChunks.intro: The confession didn't go the way I hoped, {player_name}. The truth came out, and now everyone knows.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When I admitted what I did to the council in {location}, the reaction was exactly what I deserved. They stripped my standing and half the settlement won't look at me. But {target_npc} heard about the confession from {location_hint} and sent word: not forgiveness, but a request. They need {quest_item} to defend against the {enemy_type} that have been threatening their camp. If I can deliver what they asked for, it's not redemption, but it's a start. The problem is the road there is crawling with {quest_threat}.
dialogueChunks.outro: The settlement turned its back on me. But {target_npc} opened a door, just a crack. I won't waste it.

playerResponses.text: A crack in the door is enough. I'll help you push through it.
You lost everything and they're still asking for help. At least they're talking. I'll go.
You burned every bridge. Walking through fire won't rebuild them.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the past just caught up with us in the worst possible way.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} is in danger. The {enemy_type} overran the area {location_hint} where they've been living, and they sent a desperate message asking for help. The irony isn't lost on me: the person I wronged is now begging for rescue from the person who caused their exile. I want to go myself, but I know my presence would do more harm than good right now. I need you to bring {quest_item} and clear the {enemy_type} from the area so {target_npc} can escape safely. If they survive because of me, maybe that starts to balance the scales.
dialogueChunks.outro: Saving someone you hurt isn't redemption. But letting them die when you could help is a sin beyond any I've already committed.

playerResponses.text: No one deserves to be abandoned twice. I'll get them out.
The person you wronged needs rescue by proxy. There's a painful irony in that.
They're in danger because you got them exiled. This isn't my mess.
